title: R (on the application of Maguire) (Appellant) v His Majesty’s Senior Coroner for Blackpool & Fylde and another (Respondents)

metadata.title: R (on the application of Maguire) (Appellant) v His Majesty’s Senior Coroner for Blackpool & Fylde and another (Respondents)
metadata.summary: Principle issue: Whether the death of a disabled woman who was deprived of her liberty engaged the state’s obligation to protect life under Article 2 of the European Convention on Human Rights, therefore requiring an inquest jury to make findings regarding the circumstances by which the death occurred.
extracts: 1. This case is concerned with the conduct of an inquest into the death of Ms Jacqueline Maguire, aged 52, in hospital on 22 February 2017 from pneumonia and a perforated gastric ulcer and peritonitis and the impact upon this of article 2 of the European Convention on Human Rights (“the Convention”). Article 2 is a convention right set out in the Human Rights Act 1998 (“the HRA”). I will refer to Jacqueline Maguire as “Jackie”, as her family has requested.

2. Article 2 provides, so far as is relevant, that “Everyone’s right to life shall be protected by law”. It is established law that this provision has a substantive aspect, governing the ways in which the state should act to protect life, and a procedural aspect, which imposes an obligation on the state to provide for investigation as to whether a death may have resulted from a breach of the substantive obligations imposed by article 2. The precise content of the substantive obligations and of the procedural obligation under article 2 varies depending on the circumstances of a particular case. In this appeal, both the substantive aspect and the procedural aspect of article 2 are in issue.
metadata.title_key: r v his majesty s senior coroner for blackpool fylde and another
